reasoning: To reach between $500 billion and $1 trillion in customs duties in FY 2025, collections would need to increase by more than 6x compared to the $82.2 billion collected in FY 2024. FY 2025 runs from October 1, 2024 to September 30, 2025. The Biden administration maintains the status quo through January 20, 2025, leaving only about 8 months under a new administration's policies. Even if a hypothetical Trump administration implemented an immediate 20% universal tariff on completely unshielded goods on day one, applying this to the expected ~$2 trillion in imports over the remaining 8 months of the fiscal year would barely reach the lower end of the $500b threshold on paper. In reality, legal challenges, implementation lag, exemptions, forward-loading of imports by companies ahead of January 20th, and a significant drop in import demand (elasticity) would severely suppress actual tax collections well below the $500 billion mark. Thus, the probability is practically non-existent.
